basicDetails.implementingAgency: Secondary Education Department, Government Of Uttarakhand

schemeContent.detailedDescription_md: The “NIPUN Bharat Mission” is a scheme under the Samagra Shiksha Programme, implemented by the Secondary Education Department, Government of Uttarakhand. It aims to ensure that all children aged 3 to 9 achieve foundational literacy and numeracy by creating an enabling learning environment across all schools in Uttarakhand.
schemeContent.benefits_md: Under this mission, all children can get admission in Class 1 as per the admission norms of all schools in the state.
schemeContent.briefDescription: The scheme aims to ensure that all children aged 3 to 9 achieve foundational literacy and numeracy by creating an enabling learning environment across all schools in Uttarakhand.

children.text: The “NIPUN Bharat Mission” is a scheme under the Samagra Shiksha Programme, implemented by the Secondary Education Department, Government of Uttarakhand. It aims to ensure that all children aged 3 to 9 achieve foundational literacy and numeracy by creating an enabling learning environment across all schools in Uttarakhand.
Under this mission, all children can get admission in Class 1 as per the admission norms of all schools in the state.

The main objective of the Nipun Bharat Mission is to create an enabling environment to ensure universal attainment of basic literacy and numeracy. Admissions can be obtained in Class 1 as per admission norms in all schools of the state.

applicationProcess.process_md: The main objective of the Nipun Bharat Mission is to create an enabling environment to ensure universal attainment of basic literacy and numeracy. Admissions can be obtained in Class 1 as per admission norms in all schools of the state.

eligibilityCriteria.eligibilityDescription_md: 1. The beneficiary must be a resident of Uttarakhand. 1. All children aged 3 to 9 years are eligible to benefit from the scheme to achieve foundational literacy and numeracy.

children.text: The beneficiary must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
All children aged 3 to 9 years are eligible to benefit from the scheme to achieve foundational literacy and numeracy.
